verdict_reasoning: HSPD1/HSP60 is canonically a mitochondrial chaperonin, but it has well-documented cell-state-induced surface translocation. Under stress, heat shock, infection, apoptosis, and oncogenic transformation, HSP60 relocates to the plasma membrane outer leaflet, where it acts as a signaling/innate-immune molecule accessible to extracellular ligands, antibodies, and immune cells. Multiple antibody and immunotherapy studies have targeted surface HSP60 on tumor and stressed cells. This surface form is context-dependent (not the baseline mitochondrial localization), qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced.
